Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

What is the thing you like the most about ISKCON?

Social · asked by user [] · 2009-03-19 · 10 answers
Because bad there are many but you have to say at least one good thing.
user [2] · 2009-03-19
.... hmmmm, difficult question ...
the name, it is Srila Prabhupada'b4s genius!
user [72] · 2009-03-19
Prabhupada
user [366] · 2009-03-19
ISKCON, or just Gaudiya Vaishnavism in general, because ISKCON is a branch (a special one at that) of GV.

In any case, I like the variety of foodstuffs, and thanks to ISKCON, we have more than just roti and subjis, haha...

ISKCON cooks ki JAYA!

Edit: And of course, Prabhupada and the English books are a given :)
user [19] · 2009-03-19
I like the logo.
user [23] · 2009-03-19
Harinam sankirtan, especially in public.
user [380] · 2009-03-19
Prabhupada, Parampara, Deity Worship, Harinaam Sankirtan, Panchtattva, n My most beloved GuruMaharaj
user [33] · 2009-03-19
Prabhupada, Prabhupadas books
user [166] · 2009-03-20
The chance to get free from material consciousness...without Srila Prabhupadas society of devotees - all of us would still be 100% engrossed in material consciousness without access to the chanting of the Holy names.
user [23] · 2009-03-20
[quote][cite] rasa108:[/cite]The chance to get free from material consciousness...without Srila Prabhupadas society of devotees - all of us would still be 100% engrossed in material consciousness without access to the chanting of the Holy names.[/quote]

I was absorbed in mayavadi philosophy (mostly Vasisthas Yoga, http://vedabase.net/cc/adi/12/40/en2), on my way to go live as a hermit in the forest, when Bhagavad-gita As It Is rescued me. 8^) That may be why Im such a difficult case for cultivating bhakti.
user [418] · 2009-11-28
All glories to Srila Prabhupada and beautiful Sri Murthis in the Temples, Harinam sankirtan, mahaprasadam and prasadam feasts, Srila Prabhupadas and Krishna books, Krishna Conscious family life, association with sincere devotees, KC farms, schools, restaurants, festivals--especially Lord Jagananaths Rathyatra, flower garlands, gurupuja, Mangal arati, evening arati, freedom from illicit sex, drugs and meat eating, having Temples as a destination when traveling, getting an ultimate goal for life, a constant challenge to improve the standard of ones life, becoming a much better person, and losing worry about dying, learning how great Lord Krishna is, understanding His creation and trying to become His loving servant.
